revision.comment: 新しいページ：木の岡古墳群（滋賀県大津市にある古墳群）。
revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|木の岡本塚古墳 墳丘}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=木の岡
古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|木の岡古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''木の岡古墳群'''（このおかこふんぐん）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]木の岡町にある[[古墳群]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、木の岡本塚古墳（木の岡丸山古墳）が宮内庁により「'''下坂本陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第38代[[天智天皇]][[皇后]][[倭姫王|倭姫]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されているほか、5基がその[[陪塚|陪冢]]（陪塚）に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|下坂本陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] 滋賀県南部、湖西南半において[[琵琶湖]]を望む木の岡丘陵に営造された古墳群である。丘陵上において[[前方後円墳]]1基（茶臼山）・[[帆立貝形古墳]]1基（本塚）・[[円墳]]3基（御前塚・首塚・新塚）の計5基が分布するほか、大宮川下流域の平地において円墳2基（車塚・鹿道：厳密には車塚古墳群）が分布する。 古墳群のうち最高所に位置する'''本塚古墳'''（丸山古墳）は、墳丘長73メートル・高さ10メートルを測る帆立貝形古墳である{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]が認められるが、[[埴輪]]は検出されていない{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。東側の円墳3基（御前塚・首塚・新塚）は本古墳の[[陪塚|陪冢]]とする説がある{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。被葬者に関しては、時期は異なるが[[倭姫王|倭姫]]（第38代[[天智天皇]][[皇后]]）とする伝承があり、[[宮内庁]]により[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 また'''茶臼山古墳'''は、墳丘長84メートル・高さ6メートル（後円部）を測る前方後円墳である{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は1段築成{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘表面では葺石が認められるほか、少量の埴輪が認められる{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。主体部の埋葬施設は木棺直葬か粘土槨と推定される{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。[[1978年]]（[[昭和]]53年）の前方部北側における発掘調査では周濠が検出され、多量の壺形土器が出土している{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。この茶臼山古墳の前方部側には、かつて別の前方後円墳（墳丘長40メートル程度か）が存在したとされる{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。 この木の岡古墳群は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]代の営造と推定される{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。周辺では[[終末期古墳]]の平石古墳などの築造も知られ、[[日吉大社]]の存在と考え合わせて、湖南地域では唯一の首長墓群として注目される古墳群である{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。 == 一覧 == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+木の岡古墳群の一覧{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}} !古墳名 !所在地 !位置 !形状 !規模 !宮内庁治定 !備考 |- |'''本塚古墳'''
（丸山古墳）||rowspan=5|大津市木の岡町||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|44.22|N|135|52|58.87|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡本塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地）}}||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長73m||下坂本陵墓参考地|| |- |'''御前塚古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡御前塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地い号）}}||円墳|| ||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地い号|| |- |'''首塚古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡首塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地ろ号）}}||円墳|| ||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地ろ号|| |- |'''茶臼山古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡茶臼山古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地は号）}}||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長84m||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地は号||1978年発掘調査 |- |'''新塚古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡新塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地ほ号）}}||円墳|| ||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地ほ号|| |- |'''車塚古墳'''||大津市比叡辻||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡車塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地に号）}}||円墳||直径25m||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地に号||車塚古墳群
1990年度発掘調査 |- |'''鹿道古墳'''|| || ||円墳||直径14.3m|| ||車塚古墳群 |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|御前塚古墳（飛地い号） File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|首塚古墳（飛地ろ号） File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|茶臼山古墳（飛地は号） File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|新塚古墳（飛地ほ号） File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|車塚古墳（飛地に号） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（下阪本学区まちづくり推進協議会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=丸山竜平|year=1989|chapter=木の岡古墳群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=木の岡古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 25 滋賀県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/download/dayori/156.pdf 木の岡車塚古墳から埴輪を発見（滋賀文化財だより No.156）]}}|publisher=財団法人滋賀県文化財保護協会|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|滋賀文化財だより No.156|1991}}}} - リンクは公益財団法人滋賀県文化財保護協会。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=木ノ岡古墳群|title=埋蔵文化財包蔵地分布調査報告書（大津市埋蔵文化財調査報告書 2）|publisher=大津市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Konooka Kofun Group}} * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/060.html 木の岡古墳群] - 大津市歴史博物館 {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=木の岡本塚古墳 |1-1=滋賀県の古墳 |1-2=大津市の歴史 |1-3=帆立貝形古墳 |1-4=陵墓参考地 |redirect2=木の岡茶臼山古墳 |2-1=滋賀県の古墳 |2-2=大津市の歴史 |2-3=前方後円墳 |2-4=陵墓陪冢 }} {{デフォルトソート:このおかこふんくん}} [[Category:滋賀県の古墳]] [[Category:大津市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|木の岡本塚古墳 墳丘}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=木の岡
古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|木の岡古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''木の岡古墳群'''（このおかこふんぐん）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]木の岡町にある[[古墳群]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、木の岡本塚古墳（木の岡丸山古墳）が宮内庁により「'''下坂本陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第38代[[天智天皇]][[皇后]][[倭姫王|倭姫]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されているほか、5基がその[[陪塚|陪冢]]（陪塚）に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|下坂本陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] 滋賀県南部、湖西南半において[[琵琶湖]]を望む木の岡丘陵に営造された古墳群である。丘陵上において[[前方後円墳]]1基（茶臼山）・[[帆立貝形古墳]]1基（本塚）・[[円墳]]3基（御前塚・首塚・新塚）の計5基が分布するほか、大宮川下流域の平地において円墳2基（車塚・鹿道：厳密には車塚古墳群）が分布する。 古墳群のうち最高所に位置する'''本塚古墳'''（丸山古墳）は、墳丘長73メートル・高さ10メートルを測る帆立貝形古墳である{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]が認められるが、[[埴輪]]は検出されていない{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。東側の円墳3基（御前塚・首塚・新塚）は本古墳の[[陪塚|陪冢]]とする説がある{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。被葬者に関しては、時期は異なるが[[倭姫王|倭姫]]（第38代[[天智天皇]][[皇后]]）とする伝承があり、[[宮内庁]]により[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 また丘陵上最南に位置する'''茶臼山古墳'''は、墳丘長84メートル・高さ6メートル（後円部）を測る前方後円墳である{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は1段築成{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘表面では葺石が認められるほか、少量の埴輪が認められる{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。主体部の埋葬施設は木棺直葬か粘土槨と推定される{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。[[1978年]]（[[昭和]]53年）の前方部北側における発掘調査では周濠が検出され、多量の壺形土器が出土している{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。この茶臼山古墳の前方部側には、かつて別の前方後円墳（墳丘長40メートル程度か）が存在したとされる{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。 この木の岡古墳群は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]代の営造と推定される{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。周辺では[[終末期古墳]]の平石古墳などの築造も知られ、[[日吉大社]]の存在と考え合わせて、湖南地域では唯一の首長墓群として注目される古墳群である{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。 == 一覧 == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+木の岡古墳群の一覧{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}} !古墳名 !所在地 !位置 !形状 !規模 !宮内庁治定 !備考 |- |'''本塚古墳'''
（丸山古墳）||rowspan=5|大津市木の岡町||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|44.22|N|135|52|58.87|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡本塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地）}}||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長73m||下坂本陵墓参考地||2019年度測量調査 |- |'''御前塚古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡御前塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地い号）}}||円墳|| ||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地い号|| |- |'''首塚古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡首塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地ろ号）}}||円墳|| ||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地ろ号|| |- |'''茶臼山古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡茶臼山古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地は号）}}||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長84m||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地は号||1978年発掘調査 |- |'''新塚古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡新塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地ほ号）}}||円墳|| ||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地ほ号|| |- |'''車塚古墳'''||大津市比叡辻||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡車塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地に号）}}||円墳||直径25m||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地に号||車塚古墳群
1990年度発掘調査 |- |'''鹿道古墳'''|| || ||円墳||直径14.3m|| ||車塚古墳群 |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|御前塚古墳（飛地い号） File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|首塚古墳（飛地ろ号） File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|茶臼山古墳（飛地は号） File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|新塚古墳（飛地ほ号） File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|車塚古墳（飛地に号） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（下阪本学区まちづくり推進協議会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=丸山竜平|year=1989|chapter=木の岡古墳群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=木の岡古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 25 滋賀県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/download/dayori/156.pdf 木の岡車塚古墳から埴輪を発見（滋賀文化財だより No.156）]}}|publisher=財団法人滋賀県文化財保護協会|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|滋賀文化財だより No.156|1991}}}} - リンクは公益財団法人滋賀県文化財保護協会。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=木ノ岡古墳群|title=埋蔵文化財包蔵地分布調査報告書（大津市埋蔵文化財調査報告書 2）|publisher=大津市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Konooka Kofun Group}} * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/060.html 木の岡古墳群] - 大津市歴史博物館 {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=木の岡本塚古墳 |1-1=滋賀県の古墳 |1-2=大津市の歴史 |1-3=帆立貝形古墳 |1-4=陵墓参考地 |redirect2=木の岡茶臼山古墳 |2-1=滋賀県の古墳 |2-2=大津市の歴史 |2-3=前方後円墳 |2-4=陵墓陪冢 }} {{デフォルトソート:このおかこふんくん}} [[Category:滋賀県の古墳]] [[Category:大津市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|木の岡本塚古墳 墳丘}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=木の岡
古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|木の岡古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''木の岡古墳群'''（このおかこふんぐん）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]木の岡町にある[[古墳群]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、木の岡本塚古墳（木の岡丸山古墳）が宮内庁により「'''下坂本陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第38代[[天智天皇]][[皇后]][[倭姫王|倭姫]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されているほか、5基がその[[陪塚|陪冢]]（陪塚）に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|下坂本陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] 滋賀県南部、湖西南半において[[琵琶湖]]を望む木の岡丘陵に営造された古墳群である。丘陵上において[[前方後円墳]]1基（茶臼山）・[[帆立貝形古墳]]1基（本塚）・[[円墳]]3基（御前塚・首塚・新塚）の計5基が分布するほか、大宮川下流域の平地において円墳2基（車塚・鹿道：厳密には車塚古墳群）が分布する。 古墳群のうち最高所に位置する'''本塚古墳'''（丸山古墳）は、墳丘長73メートル・高さ10メートルを測る帆立貝形古墳である{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められるが、[[埴輪]]は検出されていない{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。東側の円墳3基（御前塚・首塚・新塚）は本古墳の[[陪塚|陪冢]]とする説がある{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。被葬者に関しては、時期は異なるが[[倭姫王|倭姫]]（第38代[[天智天皇]][[皇后]]）とする伝承があり、[[宮内庁]]により[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 また丘陵上最南に位置する'''茶臼山古墳'''は、墳丘長84メートル・高さ6メートル（後円部）を測る前方後円墳である{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は1段築成{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘外表では葺石が認められるほか、少量の埴輪が認められる{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。埋葬施設は木棺直葬か粘土槨と推定される{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。[[1978年]]（[[昭和]]53年）の前方部北側における発掘調査では周濠が検出され、多量の壺形土器が出土している{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。この茶臼山古墳の前方部側には、かつて別の前方後円墳（墳丘長40メートル程度か）が存在したとされる{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。 この木の岡古墳群は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]代の営造と推定される{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}。周辺では[[終末期古墳]]の平石古墳などの築造も知られ、[[日吉大社]]の存在と考え合わせて、湖南地域では唯一の首長墓群として注目される古墳群である{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}。 == 一覧 == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+木の岡古墳群の一覧{{Sfn|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}} !古墳名 !所在地 !位置 !形状 !規模 !宮内庁治定 !備考 |- |'''本塚古墳'''
（丸山古墳）||rowspan=5|大津市木の岡町||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|44.22|N|135|52|58.87|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡本塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地）}}||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長73m||下坂本陵墓参考地||2019年度測量調査 |- |'''御前塚古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡御前塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地い号）}}||円墳|| ||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地い号|| |- |'''首塚古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡首塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地ろ号）}}||円墳|| ||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地ろ号|| |- |'''茶臼山古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡茶臼山古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地は号）}}||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長84m||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地は号||1978年発掘調査 |- |'''新塚古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡新塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地ほ号）}}||円墳|| ||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地ほ号|| |- |'''車塚古墳'''||大津市比叡辻||{{ウィキ座標|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|位置|name=木の岡車塚古墳（下坂本陵墓参考地飛地に号）}}||円墳||直径25m||下坂本陵墓参考地飛地に号||車塚古墳群
1990年度発掘調査 |- |'''鹿道古墳'''|| || ||円墳||直径14.3m|| ||車塚古墳群 |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|御前塚古墳（飛地い号） File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|首塚古墳（飛地ろ号） File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|茶臼山古墳（飛地は号） File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|新塚古墳（飛地ほ号） File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|車塚古墳（飛地に号） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（下阪本学区まちづくり推進協議会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=丸山竜平|year=1989|chapter=木の岡古墳群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|木の岡古墳群（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=木の岡古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 25 滋賀県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|木の岡古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/download/dayori/156.pdf 木の岡車塚古墳から埴輪を発見（滋賀文化財だより No.156）]}}|publisher=財団法人滋賀県文化財保護協会|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|滋賀文化財だより No.156|1991}}}} - リンクは公益財団法人滋賀県文化財保護協会。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=木ノ岡古墳群|title=埋蔵文化財包蔵地分布調査報告書（大津市埋蔵文化財調査報告書 2）|publisher=大津市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Konooka Kofun Group}} * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/060.html 木の岡古墳群] - 大津市歴史博物館 {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=木の岡本塚古墳 |1-1=滋賀県の古墳 |1-2=大津市の歴史 |1-3=帆立貝形古墳 |1-4=陵墓参考地 |redirect2=木の岡茶臼山古墳 |2-1=滋賀県の古墳 |2-2=大津市の歴史 |2-3=前方後円墳 |2-4=陵墓陪冢 }} {{デフォルトソート:このおかこふんくん}} [[Category:滋賀県の古墳]] [[Category:大津市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

Created page with "[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}..."
revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|associated burial mounds|ja|陪塚}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|round burial mounds|ja|円墳}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''

tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|associated burial mounds|ja|陪塚}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|round burial mounds|ja|円墳}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Chausuyama Kofun (Subsite "ha") File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Shinzuka Kofun (Subsite "ho") File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kurumazuka Kofun (Subsite Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Protection Association. == Related Publications == {{small|Sources not used for writing this article}} * {{Cite book|title=Report on Buried Cultural Property Sites (Otsu City Archaeological Reports 2)|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Konooka Kofun Group|publisher=Otsu City Education Committee|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Konooka Kofun Group}} Prefecture |1-2=History of Otsu City |1-3=Scallop-shaped tumuli |1-4=Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites |redirect2=Konooka Chausuyama Kofun |2-1=Kofun in Shiga Prefecture |2-2=History of Otsu City |2-3=Keyhole-shaped tumuli |2-4=Associated Burial Mounds }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Konooka Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Otsu City]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|associated burial mounds|ja|陪塚}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|round burial mounds|ja|円墳}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''

tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite "ro") File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Chausuyama Kofun (Subsite "ha") File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Shinzuka Kofun (Subsite "ho") File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kurumazuka Kofun (Subsite The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|associated burial mounds|ja|陪塚}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|round burial mounds|ja|円墳}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite "ro") File:Konooka Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! revision.comment: Adding [[Category:kk]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|associated burial mounds|ja|陪塚}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|round burial mounds|ja|円墳}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''

tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite "ro") File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Chausuyama Kofun (Subsite "ha") File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Shinzuka Kofun (Subsite "ho") File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kurumazuka Kofun (Subsite No.156|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Protection Association|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Shiga Cultural Property Newsletter No.156|1991}}}} - Link via revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|associated burial mounds|ja|陪塚}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|round burial mounds|ja|円墳}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''
(Maruyama Kofun)||rowspan=5|Otsu City, Konooka-cho||{{coord|35|4|44.22|N|135|52|58.87|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Motozuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite "ro") File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Chausuyama Kofun (Subsite "ha") File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Shinzuka Kofun (Subsite "ho") File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kurumazuka Kofun (Subsite (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 25 - Shiga Prefecture|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=Konooka Kofun Group|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shiga Cultural Property Newsletter 木の岡古墳群
[[Category:kk]][[Category:木の岡古墳群]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ki-no-oka Moto-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ki-no-oka Moto-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ki-no-oka Moto-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ki-no-oka Moto-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:01-17]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]]
{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Ki-no-oka Moto-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:木の岡古墳群]]

[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|associated burial mounds|ja|陪塚}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|round burial mounds|ja|円墳}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''

tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite "ro") File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Chausuyama Kofun (Subsite "ha") File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Shinzuka Kofun (Subsite "ho") File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kurumazuka Kofun (Subsite (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 25 - Shiga Prefecture|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=Konooka Kofun Group|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shiga Cultural Property Newsletter (Otsu City Archaeological Reports 2)|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Konooka Kofun Group|publisher=Otsu City Education Committee|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Konooka Kofun Group}} Prefecture |1-2=History of Otsu City |1-3=Scallop-shaped tumuli |1-4=Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites |redirect2=Konooka Chausuyama Kofun |2-1=Kofun in Shiga Prefecture |2-2=History of Otsu City |2-3=Keyhole-shaped tumuli |2-4=Associated Burial Mounds }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Konooka Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Otsu City]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|associated burial mounds|ja|陪塚}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|round burial mounds|ja|円墳}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite "ro") File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Chausuyama Kofun (Subsite "ha") File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Shinzuka Kofun (Subsite "ho") File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kurumazuka Kofun (Subsite 木の岡古墳群
[[Category:kk]][[Category:木の岡古墳群]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ki-no-oka Moto-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ki-no-oka Moto-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ki-no-oka Moto-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ki-no-oka Moto-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:01-17]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]]

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Ki-no-oka Moto-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:木の岡古墳群]]

[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|associated burial mounds|ja|陪塚}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|round burial mounds|ja|円墳}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''

tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite "ro") File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Chausuyama Kofun (Subsite "ha") File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Shinzuka Kofun (Subsite "ho") File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kurumazuka Kofun (Subsite
