Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''
(Maruyama Kofun)||rowspan=5|Otsu City, Konooka-cho||{{coord|35|4|44.22|N|135|52|58.87|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Motozuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite "ro") File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Chausuyama Kofun (Subsite "ha") File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Shinzuka Kofun (Subsite "ho") File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kurumazuka Kofun (Subsite [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka revision.comment: Text replacement - "|古墳}}" to "|古墳|1=Kofun}}"
revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''
(Maruyama Kofun)||rowspan=5|Otsu City, Konooka-cho||{{coord|35|4|44.22|N|135|52|58.87|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Motozuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite "ro") File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Chausuyama Kofun (Subsite "ha") File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Shinzuka Kofun (Subsite "ho") File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kurumazuka Kofun (Subsite (Otsu City Archaeological Reports 2)|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Konooka Kofun Group|publisher=Otsu City Education Committee|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Konooka Kofun Group}} [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''
(Maruyama Kofun)||rowspan=5|Otsu City, Konooka-cho||{{coord|35|4|44.22|N|135|52|58.87|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Motozuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Removing this excess]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''
(Maruyama Kofun)||rowspan=5|Otsu City, Konooka-cho||{{coord|35|4|44.22|N|135|52|58.87|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Motozuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite "ro") File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Chausuyama Kofun (Subsite "ha") File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Shinzuka Kofun (Subsite "ho") File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kurumazuka Kofun (Subsite (Otsu City Archaeological Reports 2)|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Konooka Kofun Group|publisher=Otsu City Education Committee|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Konooka Kofun Group}} Prefecture |1-2=History of Otsu City |1-3=Scallop-shaped tumuli |1-4=Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites |redirect2=Konooka Chausuyama Kofun |2-1=Kofun in Shiga Prefecture |2-2=History of Otsu City [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''

tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''

tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite "ro") File:Konooka (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 25 - Shiga Prefecture|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=Konooka Kofun Group|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shiga Cultural Property Newsletter (Otsu City Archaeological Reports 2)|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Konooka Kofun Group|publisher=Otsu City Education Committee|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Konooka Kofun Group}} Prefecture |1-2=History of Otsu City |1-3=Scallop-shaped tumuli |1-4=Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites |redirect2=Konooka Chausuyama Kofun |2-1=Kofun in Shiga Prefecture |2-2=History of Otsu City The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! revision.text:
[[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Otsu|ja|大津市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天智天皇}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated burial mounds}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=round burial mounds}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round burial mounds (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated burial mounds of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in [[1978]] (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Mausoleum Reference Site||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka
