revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Konooka Kofun Group]][[ja:木の岡古墳群]][[zh:木之岡古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Konooka Kofun Group]][[ja:木の岡古墳群]][[zh:木之岡古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Konooka Kofun Group]][[ja:木の岡古墳群]][[zh:木之岡古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q66571344]] Wikidata name: Konooka Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q66571344]] Wikidata name: Konooka Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|WD=Q202907|lt=Otsu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Yamato-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4388732}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor of Japan}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=round Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round Kofuns (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated Kofuns of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in {{ill|1978|en|1978|WD=Q2483|lt=1978}} (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''
(Maruyama Kofun)||rowspan=5|Otsu City, Konooka-cho||{{coord|35|4|44.22|N|135|52|58.87|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Motozuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite "ro") File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Chausuyama Kofun (Subsite "ha") File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Shinzuka Kofun (Subsite "ho") File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 25 - Shiga revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Konooka Kofun Group]][[ja:木の岡古墳群]][[zh:木之岡古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Konooka Kofun Group]][[ja:木の岡古墳群]][[zh:木之岡古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Konooka Kofun Group]][[ja:木の岡古墳群]][[zh:木之岡古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}}}} [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|WD=Q202907|lt=Otsu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Yamato-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4388732}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor of Japan}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=round Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round Kofuns (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated Kofuns of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in {{ill|1978|en|1978|WD=Q2483|lt=1978}} (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''
(Maruyama Kofun)||rowspan=5|Otsu City, Konooka-cho||{{coord|35|4|44.22|N|135|52|58.87|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Motozuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum||Surveyed in 2019 |- |'''Gozenzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|46.25|N|135|53|3.68|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Gozenzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ro")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ro"|| |- |'''Chausuyama Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || ||Round mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite "ro") File:Konooka Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Chausuyama Kofun (Subsite "ha") File:Konooka Shinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Shinzuka Kofun (Subsite "ho") File:Konooka Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kurumazuka Kofun (Subsite "ni") == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for writing this article}} * Information board (installed by the Shimosakamoto Neighborhood Planning Council) * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|editor=|author=Ryūhei Maruyama|year=1989|chapter=Konooka Kofun Group|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Topography Series, Volume 25 - Shiga Prefecture|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=Konooka Kofun Group|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shiga Cultural Property Newsletter No.156|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Protection Association|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|Shiga Cultural Property Newsletter No.156|1991}}}} - Link via Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Protection Association. == Related Publications == {{small|Sources not used for writing this article}} * {{Cite book|title=Report on Buried Cultural Property Sites (Otsu City Archaeological Reports 2)|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Konooka Kofun Group|publisher=Otsu City Education Committee|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Konooka Kofun Group}} * [http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/060.html Konooka Kofun Group] - Otsu City Museum of History {{Redirect category affiliation |redirect1=Konooka Motozuka Kofun Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|WD=Q202907|lt=Otsu|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Yamato-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4388732}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor Tenji}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=round Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round Kofuns (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated Kofuns of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in {{ill|1978|en|1978|WD=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}} (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''
(Maruyama Kofun)||rowspan=5|Otsu City, Konooka-cho||{{coord|35|4|44.22|N|135|52|58.87|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Motozuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum)}}||{{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Subsite "i")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "i"|| |- |'''Kubizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|42.72|N|135|53|1.32|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kubizuka Kofun Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|35.42|N|135|52|59.40|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Chausuyama Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ha")}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum mound||Diameter: 14.3m|| ||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster |} File:Konooka Gozenzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Gozenzuka Kofun (Subsite "i") File:Konooka Kubizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kubizuka Kofun (Subsite Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Yamato-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4388732}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor Tenji}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=round Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round Kofuns (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated Kofuns of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in {{ill|1978|en|1978|WD=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}} (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''

Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=round Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round Kofuns (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated Kofuns of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in {{ill|1978|en|1978|WD=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}} (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! Kofun Name ! Location ! Coordinates ! Shape ! Dimensions ! Imperial Designation ! Remarks |- |'''Motozuka Kofun'''

tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|qid=Q202907|lt=Otsu|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Yamato-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4388732}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor Tenji}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated Kofuns of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in {{ill|1978|en|1978|qid=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}} (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|qid=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated Kofuns of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in {{ill|1978|en|1978|qid=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}} (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|qid=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||Mound length: 73m||Shimosakamoto revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Konooka Kofun Group]][[ja:木の岡古墳群]][[zh:木之岡古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Konooka Kofun Group]][[ja:木の岡古墳群]][[zh:木之岡古墳群]] [[ja:木の岡古墳群]][[zh:木之岡古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Otsu|qid=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}}} [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|qid=Q202907|lt=Otsu|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Yamato-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4388732}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor Tenji}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=round Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round Kofuns (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated Kofuns of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in {{ill|1978|en|1978|qid=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}} (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|qid=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! "ha")}}||{{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}||Mound length: 84m||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ha"||Excavated in 1978 |- |'''Shinzuka Kofun'''||{{coord|35|4|45.13|N|135|53|4.85|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Shinzuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ho")}}||Round mound|| ||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ho"|| |- |'''Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Otsu City, Hiezuji||{{coord|35|4|27.77|N|135|52|53.70|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|Kurumazuka Kofun (Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ni")}}||Round mound||Diameter: 25m||Shimosakamoto Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Subsite "ni"||Part of Kurumazuka Kofun Cluster; excavated in 1990 |- |'''Shikamichi Kofun'''|| || The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in {{ill|1978|en|1978|qid=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}} (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|qid=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} ! revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Otsu|qid=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}}} [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Konooka Motozuka Kofun mound}}]] {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=44.22|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=58.87|label=Konooka
Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Konooka Kofun Group}}}} {{Coordinate list}} '''Konooka Kofun Group''' (木の岡古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} cluster located in {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|qid=Q202907|lt=Otsu|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. Although the actual burial occupants are unclear, the Konooka Motozuka Kofun (Konooka Maruyama Kofun) is designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "'''Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum'''" (candidate burial occupant: {{ill|Empress Yamato-hime|ja|倭姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Yamato-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4388732}}, consort of Emperor Tenji, the 38th {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor Tenji}}). Additionally, five other kofun are designated as its {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|Baizuka=Baizuka|lt=associated Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}. == Overview == [[File:Konooka Motozuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimosakamoto Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum Shrine}}]] The Konooka Kofun Group is situated on the Konooka hills in southern Shiga Prefecture, overlooking the southern part of Lake Biwa. The cluster comprises one {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} (Chausuyama), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Motozuka), and three {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=round Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) on the hills, along with two additional round Kofuns (Kurumazuka, Shikamichi) in the plains near the lower reaches of the Omiyagawa River. The '''Motozuka Kofun''' (Maruyama Kofun), located at the highest point, is a scallop-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 73 meters and a height of 10 meters{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound consists of two tiers{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, and {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (covering stones) have been identified on the mound's surface, although no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} were discovered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The three round mounds to the east (Gozenzuka, Kubizuka, Shinzuka) are speculated to be associated Kofuns of the Motozuka Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. The burial occupant is traditionally identified as Yamato-hime, the consort of Emperor Tenji, based on historical accounts, and the Imperial Household Agency has designated the site as a {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. The '''Chausuyama Kofun''', located at the southernmost point of the hills, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 84 meters and a height of 6 meters (rear circular section){{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound is single-tiered{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}, with fukiishi and a small number of haniwa identified{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a direct burial in a wooden coffin or a clay-coffin-type structure{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Excavations on the northern side of the front section in {{ill|1978|en|1978|qid=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}} (Showa 53) uncovered a surrounding moat and numerous jar-shaped pottery pieces{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Another smaller keyhole-shaped tumulus (approximately 40 meters in mound length) is believed to have once existed near the front section of Chausuyama Kofun{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. The Konooka Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the mid-5th century in the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Nearby, other tumuli such as the Hiraiseki Kofun from the terminal period are also known. Together with the presence of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|qid=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}, the Konooka Kofun Group is notable as the only known cluster of chief tombs in the southern Lake Biwa region{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}}. == List of Konooka Kofun Group == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+List of Konooka Kofun Group{{Sfn|Konooka Kofun Group (Kofun)|1989}} !
